---
title: "Test methods protocol — medRxiv flag fixture"
author:
  - name: Test Author
    affiliations:
      - id: ohri
        name: "Ottawa Hospital Research Institute"
abstract: |
  **Background:** Wastewater surveillance has emerged as a useful
  public health tool. This protocol describes a scoping review.

  **Methods:** PRISMA-ScR-guided scoping review with LLM-assisted
  title and abstract screening. PCC framework, multi-database search.

  **Findings:** Will be summarised on completion.

  **Conclusions:** This protocol pre-registers the methodology before
  screening begins.
---

# Background

This is a synthetic test fixture for the medRxiv flag on the
docstyle-typst format. It exercises the line-number, single-column,
1-inch-margin, and PDF/UA-1 defaults applied when the flag is on.

The body text exists so the rendered PDF has enough content for
text extraction tools to pull and verify in tests.

# Methods

The protocol follows PRISMA-ScR guidance. Population, Concept, and
Context (PCC) elements are defined. The search strategy uses MeSH and
free-text terms across PubMed, Embase, and Web of Science.

LLM-assisted screening uses a staged architecture: open-weights models
for clear cases, escalation to stronger models for ambiguous ones.

# Competing interests

None.

# Funding

This is a test fixture; no funding statement is real.

# Data availability

This fixture lives at inst/extdata/medrxiv-fixture/ in the docstyle
package source.
